message:
feat(runtime,command,store): add MULTI arity validation, command arity checker, randomized eviction, and trim score parsing

- Add check_command_arity() to fr-command that validates argument count
  against the COMMAND_TABLE arity spec (exact or minimum), returning the
  command name on mismatch for error formatting
- Wire arity check into MULTI transaction queueing in fr-runtime: reject
  wrong-arity commands immediately with ERR and set EXECABORT, matching
  Redis behavior where malformed commands taint the transaction before
  EXEC runs
- Add multi_rejects_wrong_arity_immediately and
  multi_queues_valid_commands_after_arity_rejection tests
- Replace deterministic sorted-first-key eviction in AllkeysRandom and
  VolatileRandom with next_rand()-based random index selection, providing
  actual random eviction semantics matching Redis behavior
- Add .trim() to parse_score_bound and ZUNIONSTORE/ZINTERSTORE weight
  parsing for whitespace tolerance matching Redis inline command behavior
- Cap RDB hash field_count pre-allocation to 1024 in decode_rdb to
  prevent OOM on malformed RDB payloads
- Update EXISTING_REDIS_STRUCTURE.md UZ-001/002/003 descriptions to
  reflect current ACL, persistence, and expire/eviction implementation
  baseline

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -751,9 +751,9 @@ Source anchors:
 
 | Zone ID | Zone summary | Why this is security/compat sensitive | Current state in Rust | Contract/evidence anchor |
 |---|---|---|---|---|
-| UZ-001 | ACL/authorization parity beyond basic auth | startup source ambiguity, selector grammar, and ACL command semantics are critical policy boundaries | runtime has basic `AUTH`/`HELLO AUTH` gate, but ACL command family/selector model and transactional ACL load surfaces are missing | `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:401`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-004/legacy_anchor_map.md:20`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-004/legacy_anchor_map.md:35` |
-| UZ-002 | Persistence manifest/replay/RDB/rewrite pipeline parity | replay ordering and tamper handling are core non-regression contracts | `fr-persist` currently provides minimal RESP AOF record encode/decode substrate only; manifest orchestration/rewrite/RDB/WAITAOF semantics remain missing | `crates/fr-persist/src/lib.rs:11`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-005/legacy_anchor_map.md:20`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-005/legacy_anchor_map.md:44` |
-| UZ-003 | Active expire + eviction policy engine | resource-exhaustion and TTL/eviction ordering must stay deterministic and fail-closed under stress | lazy per-key expiry exists; active-expire cycles, maxmemory accounting, eviction loops/safety gates are not yet implemented | `crates/fr-expire/src/lib.rs:10`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-008/implementation_plan.md:32`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-008/legacy_anchor_map.md:21` |
+| UZ-001 | ACL/authorization parity beyond basic auth | startup source ambiguity, selector grammar, and ACL command semantics are critical policy boundaries | runtime baseline now includes full ACL lifecycle commands, selector/category/command permissions, dispatch-time authorization, and `core_acl` conformance coverage; remaining gap is deeper parity around legacy ACL-file/packet docs and long-tail policy semantics | `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`, `FEATURE_PARITY.md:23`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-004/legacy_anchor_map.md:20` |
+| UZ-002 | Persistence manifest/replay/RDB/rewrite pipeline parity | replay ordering and tamper handling are core non-regression contracts | persistence baseline now includes AOF replay, rewrite serialization, SAVE/BGSAVE/BGREWRITEAOF wiring, RDB encode/decode, and `persist_replay` coverage; remaining gap is deeper recovery-ordering parity and downstream packet/source-doc alignment | `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`, `FEATURE_PARITY.md:21`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-005/legacy_anchor_map.md:20` |
+| UZ-003 | Active expire + eviction policy engine | resource-exhaustion and TTL/eviction ordering must stay deterministic and fail-closed under stress | active-expire cycles, maxmemory classification/enforcement, and eviction-policy support are present in the current baseline; remaining gap is broader Redis edge-semantic parity and packet/source-doc alignment around FR-P2C-008 | `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`, `crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs`, `FEATURE_PARITY.md:20` |
 | UZ-004 | Full replication command/runtime surface | handshake/order attacks and stale-stream replay depend on complete state-machine plumbing | command/runtime baseline now includes `SYNC`/`PSYNC`/`REPLCONF`/`WAIT`/`WAITAOF`, backlog/offset accounting, full-resync snapshot apply, and reconnect/partial-resync flow; remaining gap is broader legacy-oracle edge semantics and downstream packet/source-doc alignment | `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`, `crates/fr-server/src/main.rs`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-006/legacy_anchor_map.md:25` |
 | UZ-005 | Cluster route/failover/admin parity | slot ownership, redirect shaping, and cluster bus parsing are high-risk correctness/security boundaries | runtime currently exposes only minimal `CLUSTER HELP` scaffold and client mode toggles; slot/routing/failover/bus/admin surfaces remain undefined in Rust | `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:499`, `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:519`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-007/legacy_anchor_map.md:22` |
 | UZ-006 | TLS runtime handshake/listener orchestration parity | downgrade/conflicting-config paths require deterministic fail-closed runtime behavior | config validation and policy gates exist, but runtime handshake/listener parity and persistence-linked rewrite paths are still contractual | `crates/fr-config/src/lib.rs:460`, `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:239`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-009/risk_note.md:145` |
@@ -766,7 +766,7 @@ Source anchors:
 | Legacy behavior missing in Rust | cluster packet parser/failover reducers | fail closed, emit deterministic reason code, keep packet blocked from promotion |
 | Partial implementation with contractual intent | expire helper exists without scheduler integration | document as non-parity substrate; prohibit parity claims beyond implemented scope |
 | Contract docs ahead of implementation | packet-level hardened exhaustion reason codes listed in risk/contract tables | track as explicit implementation debt; no silent fallback to undocumented behavior |
-| Doc-vs-code drift | legacy map entries claiming missing `AUTH` while runtime contains minimal auth flow | mark as stale contract artifact and reconcile in packet artifacts before closure |
+| Doc-vs-code drift | legacy map entries claiming missing or basic-only auth while runtime now contains broad ACL/auth support | mark as stale contract artifact and reconcile packet/source docs before closure |
 
 ### 21.5 Priority order for edge-case closure (documentation gating view)
